top_posts.author: Construction Business News Middle East
top_posts.title: GCC Construction Leadership: Crisis Management Steps - LinkedIn
top_posts.preview: 23 March 2026 ... He implements high-impact PMO frameworks that turn digital transformation and risk governance into measurable growth.
top_posts.draft_comment: Most crisis frameworks for construction focus on cash flow and contract clauses, but the hidden vulnerability in GCC mega-projects is supply chain concentration. With a significant portion of specialist materials and skilled labour flowing through narrow corridors, a single geopolitical friction point can freeze multiple sites simultaneously. The leaders who navigated 2016-2020 best weren't those with the biggest cash reserves - they were those with geographic diversification in their supplier base. How are you currently mapping second and third-tier supplier exposure across your programme?

top_posts.author: Saudi Gazette
top_posts.title: Saudi Arabia Empowers 666,000 Women in Data & AI - LinkedIn
top_posts.preview: Saudi Arabia has strengthened its global presence in empowering women in the data and artificial intelligence sector by training more than
top_posts.draft_comment: The headline number is striking, but I would push past the quantity metric to ask what "equipped" means in practice. Training 666,000 women on AI concepts is a powerful signal, but the real measure of success will be how many move from awareness into production roles, building and deploying models rather than using them. Saudi's bet on deep technical upskilling will define whether this becomes a workforce transformation or an impressive awareness campaign. What does the graduation-to-employment pipeline look like?

top_posts.author: Pro Partner Group
top_posts.title: #eidmubarak #gcc #businesssupport #middleeast | Sovereign PPG
top_posts.preview: ... Monday, 23rd March 2026 KSA – Closed 19th to 24th March | Opening Wednesday, 25th March 2026 Qatar – Closed 19th to 23rd March
top_posts.draft_comment: Eid Mubarak to all. A genuine question this always raises for me: the region loses significant project velocity during holiday periods, and in construction and PMO contexts, the coordination cost of shutting down and restarting multi-contractor programmes often gets undercounted. What is your approach to maintaining momentum across the holiday without burning out your teams?

top_posts.author: Guillaume Claude oneill
top_posts.title: Desalination Plants in Dubai: Critical Infrastructure at Risk - LinkedIn
top_posts.preview: Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Kuwait, Qatar, these nations have mastered the science of turning seawater into drinking water at a scale the world has
top_posts.draft_comment: The operational risk from desalination dependency is well understood, but the geopolitical dimension is what keeps me up at night. Any disruption to feedstock supply, energy inputs, or the specialized chemical supply chain for these plants creates a days-level crisis in a city of 3 million with zero freshwater alternatives. GCC governments know this, which partly explains the aggressive diversification push into nuclear and solar. The question is whether the transition is happening fast enough given the pace of urban growth.

top_posts.author: Abhi
top_posts.title: ABHI's Post - LinkedIn
top_posts.preview: 🗓️ Monday 23 March 2026 Members only ABHI members who ... Bringing The Best of UK Healthcare Innovation to WHX Dubai. ABHI 1mo
top_posts.draft_comment: The real bottleneck in NHS HealthTech adoption is not usually clinical efficacy - it is the institutional capacity to change. NHS trusts can evaluate innovation on paper, but the operational teams needed to implement new workflows are stretched across rota gaps and competing priorities. ABHI's frameworks addressing value recognition are essential, but does the conversation also cover adoption enablement - who owns implementation inside a trust, and what does their success look like?
